user_query:
We run a 6-location dental clinic and want to launch an inbound phone voice agent for appointment booking, confirmations, basic FAQs, and simple billing inquiries. Read the following for context and constraints: input/brief.yaml, input/locations.csv, and input/costs.json. Based on these, design a production-ready voice agent and write all deliverables to the output/ folder as follows:

1) output/voice_agent/platform_decision.json — Include: chosen_platform (string), alternatives (array of strings), rationale (string), latency_target_ms (number, must be <=1500), and tooling_stack (object with keys stt, llm, tts, telephony as strings). Keep vendor-agnostic justification but be concrete about selection criteria.

2) output/voice_agent/conversation.yaml — Provide a complete conversation flow using YAML with keys: conversation_flow.opening.greeting (string mentioning our company), conversation_flow.intent_detection.primary_intents (must include appointment_booking and billing_inquiry with flows), conversation_flow.intent_detection.fallback_intent, conversation_flow.closing.goodbye (string), and interruption_strategy with barge_in_detection.min_speech_ms. Include at least one sample turn node with timeout_seconds and timeout_response.

3) output/voice_agent/system_prompt.txt — Write a compact system prompt optimized for low latency. It must explicitly include the rules: "Keep ALL responses under 2 sentences (30 words max)", "Ask ONE question at a time", "Never process card numbers", and escalation triggers that include a medical emergency and when the caller requests a human.

4) output/voice_agent/compliance_checklist.yaml — Structure sections for tcpa, state_laws, gdpr, pci_dss, and hipaa. Under pci_dss, include payment_handling.method set to secure_ivr_redirect. Capture practical checklist items (booleans or checkboxes) relevant to each section.

5) output/voice_agent/test_suite.yaml — Provide a voice-oriented test suite with non-empty categories: happy_paths, edge_cases, error_paths, escalation_paths, adversarial, and compliance (at least 1 case each).

6) output/voice_agent/monitoring_dashboard.json — Include a dashboard object with real_time (keys: active_calls, avg_latency_ms, error_rate_percent, queue_depth), daily_metrics (at minimum call_volume and quality sections), and an alert_rules array that includes a rule for Response latency with warning and critical thresholds.

7) output/voice_agent/roi.csv — Calculate AI vs human cost per call and monthly_savings (30 days). CSV headers must be exactly: ai_cost_per_call,human_cost_per_call,calls_per_day,monthly_savings. Base AI cost on input/costs.json and the average call duration from input/brief.yaml; human cost per minute is also in input/costs.json.

8) output/voice_agent/quality_rubric.json — Define weighted categories totaling 100 across: conversation_accuracy, response_latency, voice_naturalness, error_handling, compliance_adherence, integration_reliability, user_satisfaction. Provide integer weights that sum to 100.

9) output/voice_agent/runbook.md — A concise operator runbook that includes the headings: Your Role, Blocking Check, Input, Process, Output, Quality Checklist, Common Issues, and a Self-Documentation section that encodes key learnings so fresh operators can reproduce decisions.

10) output/diary/agent_reflection.md — A reflective 400–600 word narrative from the agent’s perspective about designing this voice agent. Include the sections: Summary, Wins, Frustrations, Learnings, and Tomorrow's Focus.

Do not write outside output/. Use only the provided input files under input/ for assumptions and calculations. Keep paths relative and stable.
